component.name: Thermal Canary
component.summary: GPU and CPU thermal gauges for Linux gamers

description.p: Thermal Canary is a fullscreen hardware monitor for Linux that displays six circular arc gauges in real time: CPU temperature, CPU usage, and RAM on the top row; GPU temperature, GPU fan speed, and GPU VRAM on the bottom row.
Designed for gamers and power users who want a clean, always-visible thermal dashboard on a second monitor. Features include:

ul.li: Smooth 60 fps animated arc gauges with heat-map color gradient
NVIDIA (pynvml), AMD (amdgpu sysfs), and Intel GPU support
Per-monitor placement — pin it to any connected screen
Slide-in settings sidebar (Ctrl+,) for live configuration
Warn / critical thresholds with solid heat coloring
System tray icon for quick show / hide
Configurable poll rate, smoothing, colors, and GPU backend

screenshot.caption: Six thermal gauges — CPU and GPU monitoring at a glance
Slide-in settings sidebar for live configuration

description.p: Multi-monitor reliability improvements and DPMS resilience.
